strategy_reasoning: Uses the recurring cast to personify technical updates: Robot for visual models (Seedream/Gemini), Nomnom for refactoring (eating old decks), and Polly for maintenance (infra). Keeps the tone warm and community-focused rather than feature-list heavy.
visual_style: Cozy 8-bit pixel art with chunky sprites, soft CRT scanlines, and mandatory lime green (#ecf874) accents. Lo-fi Stardew Valley aesthetic.

images.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene. A cute chunky robot with a CRT monitor head is holding a glowing magical seed bulb that is projecting a holographic flower. The robot's screen eyes look amazed. Polly the bee mascot hovers nearby wearing tiny wizard glasses, watching the magic. Background is a soft gradient of lime green (#ecf874) and lavender. Warm lighting, large visible pixels, 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Text in pixel font: 'FRESH SEEDS'. Nostalgic and wholesome.
images.description: Visualizing the new Seedream and Gemini models as 'fresh seeds' being planted.

images.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene. The round tan Nomnom creature is happily eating a stack of paper documents labeled 'OLD DECK'. Beside him, Polly the bee is pointing to a retro projector screen displaying clean code blocks on a lime green (#ecf874) background. Soft peach and cream gradient atmosphere. Chunky sprites, warm glow, lo-fi vibes. Visual metaphor for replacing pitch decks with code-based presentations.
images.description: Nomnom eating the old pitch deck while Polly shows off the new Slidev (docs-as-code) setup.

images.prompt: Cozy pixel art garden shed interior. A retro server rack is covered in climbing vines. A large control panel features analog gauges and a pixelated screen showing a usage graph in lime green (#ecf874). Polly the bee is adjusting a tiny dial on the panel with a small wrench. Warm lighting, cozy atmosphere, pastel mint background. 8-bit aesthetic.
images.description: Polly tending to the infrastructure, representing the new Pollen cost dashboard.

caption:
New seeds in the garden this week. 

Added Seedream 5.0 Lite and Gemini 3.1 Flash — faster generation, smarter pixels. Swapped the corporate pitch deck for Slidev because presentations should be code (Nomnom handled the disposal of the old files). 

Also added a pollen panel to the dashboard. Transparency on costs, transparency on code. 

Build something weird with it. 🌸
alt_text: Pixel art carousel: 1. Robot and Bee looking at a magical holographic seed. 2. Round creature eating papers while Bee points to code on a screen. 3. Bee adjusting gauges in a vine-covered server room.
